content.text:
## Task: Fix [high] Code Quality: Newly added CSS test fails: quote-style mismatch between CSS selector and test regex
## Step: write-tests

You are a fix agent. Resolve the following final-review finding.

Review dimension: Code Quality
Severity: high
File: src/app/css.test.ts:261
Title: Newly added CSS test fails: quote-style mismatch between CSS selector and test regex

Finding:
The full `vitest run` suite is red: exactly 1 of 385 tests fails — `src/app.css — row action '⋮' button (.row-menu-btn) > becomes visible + interactive on row hover and when its menu is open`. The test calls `ruleDeclarations(/\.row-menu-btn\[aria-expanded="true"\]/)` (double quotes), but the corresponding rule in `src/app.css:326` is `.row-menu-btn[aria-expanded='true']` (single quotes). Because the rest of the codebase consistently uses single-quoted CSS attribute selectors (e.g. `.toolbar input[type='search']`, `input[type='text']` at lines 175–192), the CSS is correct per convention and the TEST regex is the outlier. The regex never matches, `ruleDeclarations` returns `null`, and the assertion `expect(reveal, ...).not.toBeNull()` blows up. A failing test committed to the suite blocks CI and masks future regressions. Fix the test regex to match the codebase's single-quote convention (or accept either quote style).

Fix instructions (follow exactly; make targeted, minimal changes):
In `src/app.css.test.ts`, inside the `describe('src/app.css — row action "⋮" button (.row-menu-btn)')` block, find the test titled `'becomes visible + interactive on row hover and when its menu is open'`. The line `const reveal = ruleDeclarations(/\.row-menu-btn\[aria-expanded="true"\]/);` uses double quotes, but the actual CSS rule in `src/app.css` is `.row-menu-btn[aria-expanded='true']` (single quotes, matching the codebase convention used by `.toolbar input[type='search']` etc.). Change the regex to accept the single-quote form used by the stylesheet, e.g. `const reveal = ruleDeclarations(/\.row-menu-btn\[aria-expanded='true'\]/);` (or to accept either quote style: `/\.row-menu-btn\[aria-expanded=['"]true['"]\]/`). Do not change `src/app.css`. After the edit, run `npx vitest run src/app.css.test.ts` and confirm all 20 tests in that file pass, then run `npx vitest run` and confirm 0 failures.

content.text:
The fix is complete and verified. Let me provide my structured report.

## Summary

The finding was confirmed and resolved with a minimal, targeted test-only change.

**Root cause:** The test at `src/app.css.test.ts:261` used a regex with double quotes — `/\.row-menu-btn\[aria-expanded="true"\]/` — but the actual CSS rule in `src/app.css` uses single quotes (`.row-menu-btn[aria-expanded='true']`), matching the codebase convention (e.g. `.toolbar input[type='search']`). The regex never matched, so `ruleDeclarations` returned `null`, failing the `not.toBeNull()` assertion.

**Fix applied:** Changed the regex on line 261 only to accept either quote style: `/\.row-menu-btn\[aria-expanded=['"]true['"]\]/`. This matches the single-quote convention used by the stylesheet while remaining robust.

**Constraints honored:**
- Only modified the test file `src/app.css.test.ts` (single one-line change).
- Did NOT modify `src/app.css` or any other source/production code.
- The comment (line 260) and assertion message (line 262) are descriptive prose and were left unchanged since they don't affect the test outcome.

**Verification:**
- `npx vitest run src/app.css.test.ts` → 20/20 passed.
- `npx vitest run` (full suite) → 387/387 passed, 0 failures.

The test at src/app.css.test.ts:261 used double quotes (aria-expanded=\"true\") while the actual stylesheet rule in src/app.css uses single quotes (aria-expanded='true'), matching the codebase convention. The regex never matched, so ruleDeclarations returned null and the assertion failed. Changed the regex to accept either quote style: /\\.row-menu-btn\\[aria-expanded=[\"']true[\"']\\]/.",

The descriptive comment and assertion message referencing the selector were left unchanged as they do not affect test behavior. src/app.css was intentionally not modified since the CSS is correct per the codebase's single-quote convention."
